text:
Once in the sleepy town of Elderglen, young Jack was known for his mischievous nature. His friends often teased him about his insatiable habit of pulling pranks. Among his most notorious feats was the time he filled the town’s fountain with soapy water, making everyone who drank from it feel like they’d consumed a fizzy detergent surprise.

One lazy afternoon, Jack found himself bored and daydreaming. It was late May and the sun hung high, painting the sky in hues of gold and red. This moment of boredom led to an idea as wild as the pranks Jack used to pull. He decided to replace his usual soap surprise with a harmless treasure hunt.

The next morning, as the townsfolk slowly awakened from their slumber, Jack strolled through Elderglen, wearing a small, plain hat and a sly grin. He’d hidden trinkets around the town—ranging from antique coins to beautifully engraved teaspoons—to lay out for the eager adventurers
